channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bain Capital submits non-binding takeover proposal for oOh!media amid growing private equity interest
item.description: Private equity firm Bain Capital has emerged as another potential bidder for Australian outdoor advertising company oOh!media, adding to increasing takeover interest in the ASX-listed business. Accord...

item.title: Vintage Photographs Celebrate the Beauty and Legacy of Silent Film Star Mae Murray
item.description: This article highlights the life, career, and enduring legacy of Mae Murray (1885–1965), one of the most glamorous and recognizable stars of the silent film era. Known as 'The Gardenia of the Screen,'...

item.title: Morgan Wallen Throws Security Guard’s Phone During Pittsburgh Stadium Performance
item.description: Country music star Morgan Wallen became the center of attention during a concert at Acrisure Stadium in Pittsburgh after a video surfaced showing him taking a cellphone from a security guard and throw...

item.title: Harvard Psychiatrist and Author Robert Coles Dies at 97, Noted for Work with Children in Crisis
item.description: Robert Coles, the esteemed Harvard psychiatrist and Pulitzer Prize-winning author, passed away at the age of 97 at a hospice in Lincoln, Massachusetts. Renowned for his groundbreaking work on children...

item.title: How 1980s Toy Franchises Created the Modern Transmedia Entertainment Business
item.description: The release of a new Masters of the Universe film nearly four decades after the original cartoon and 1987 movie highlights the enduring commercial power of childhood franchises. While many viewers may...

item.title: New Microraptor Species Discovered in China May Have Been a Predator of Early Birds
item.description: Researchers have identified a new species of feathered dinosaur, Jian changmaensis, from the Lower Cretaceous Xiagou Formation in China's Changma Basin. The discovery, described in the journal Annals ...

item.title: Four Security Personnel Martyred and Over 20 Injured in Rawalakot Clash Involving Banned JAAC Activists
item.description: Four law enforcement personnel were martyred and more than 20 police and security officials sustained injuries after activists associated with the banned Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JA...

item.title: The Economic Impact of Broadcast Dead Air: Lessons from 20 Years of Radio Station Outages
item.description: This article analyzes the true economic costs of dead air in radio broadcasting, drawing from two decades of real-world incident data at stations supported by KAVANA. Dead air is not merely a technica...

item.title: Indigo Stepladder Collides with Parked Air India Aircraft During Sudden Storm at Delhi Airport
item.description: On Sunday evening, a passenger stepladder used for boarding and deboarding Indigo flights was blown into a parked Air India aircraft at Delhi's Indira Gandhi International Airport due to sudden strong...

item.title: Israel Conducts Strikes on Iran Following Iranian Missile Attack Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: In a significant escalation of hostilities, Israel launched strikes on military targets in Iran after intercepting waves of ballistic missiles fired from Tehran. Explosions were reported in Iranian ci...

item.title: Rising Cybersecurity Risks in South Africa’s Healthcare Sector
item.description: South Africa has become the most targeted country in Africa for cyberattacks, with the healthcare sector facing an alarming rise in threats. Research by Unarine Jerritha Manari, a senior cybersecurity...

item.title: Nithya Raman Moves Ahead of Spencer Pratt in Los Angeles Mayoral Primary as Mail Ballots Are Counted
item.description: The article reports on a shift in the Los Angeles mayoral primary race in which Democratic Los Angeles City Councilmember Nithya Raman overtook Republican candidate and reality television personality ...

item.title: Leonard Freed’s Photographs Document NYPD Life During New York City’s Turbulent 1970s
item.description: This article showcases a collection of photographs taken by documentary photographer Leonard Freed, who closely documented the New York City Police Department during the 1970s. The images capture offi...

item.title: Sezzle's AI-Driven Product Expansion Boosts Growth Outlook but Raises Valuation Concerns
item.description: Sezzle, a buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) fintech company, has introduced a broad set of platform enhancements aimed at strengthening customer engagement and expanding its business model. The updates include...

item.title: Schmigadoon! wins Best Musical as Death of a Salesman and Ragtime collect multiple honours at the 2026 Tony Awards
item.description: The 79th Tony Awards celebrated the best productions of the 2025–2026 Broadway season at Radio City Music Hall in New York. Hosted for the first time by singer Pink, the ceremony recognised outstandin...

item.title: Nithya Raman Moves Ahead of Spencer Pratt, Likely Facing Karen Bass in Los Angeles Mayoral Runoff
item.description: Los Angeles City Councilmember Nithya Raman has overtaken reality TV personality Spencer Pratt in the city's mayoral primary, now holding second place with 27.1% of votes counted compared to Pratt's 2...

item.title: Vintage Photos Showcase the Sophisticated Beachwear Fashion of the 1930s
item.description: This article from Vintage Everyday explores the distinctive beachwear fashion that emerged during the 1930s, highlighting how seaside clothing evolved beyond simple swimwear into a sophisticated categ...

item.title: Fugitive wanted over 1999 Sydney nightclub stabbing arrested in Greece after 27 years
item.description: Greek authorities have arrested James Dalamangas, one of Australia’s long-term most wanted fugitives, nearly 27 years after he allegedly fled Sydney following a fatal stabbing at a Belmore nightclub. ...

item.title: Quetta hospital staff member awarded for saving doctor during acid attack
item.description: In Quetta, a brave act of humanity has been recognised after a female doctor, Dr Mahnoor Nisar, was attacked with acid at Civil Hospital. The incident occurred on Saturday, and the doctor was initiall...

item.title: Analyst Warns Trump’s Economic Policies Could Harm His Party Ahead of Midterms
item.description: Political analyst David Pakman highlighted concerns about President Donald Trump's approach to the U.S. economy, warning it could backfire for him and the Republican Party in the upcoming midterms. Pa...
